titleStmt.title: Berlin, Berlin State Library, Petermann II Nachtrag 24
Tārika Walda ʾAmid, Tǝrgʷāme rāʾya Yoḥannǝs

publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

supportDesc.foliation: Marked in pencil on top. Folio 95 marked twice. 95bis being in this record. Two paper endleaves at the front and two paper endleaves at the back without any foliation.

list.item:
7
s.l. 3, stub after 5 (?) Stub is not visible in the images.

8
s.l. 2, stub after 6 or after 7 (?) s.l. 7, stub after 1 or after 2 (?) Stubs are not visible in the images.

10
s.l. 7, stub after 2 s.l. 9, stub after 1

11
s.l. 8, stub after 2 (?) Collation of this quire is not described in Verzeichniss der abessinischen Handschriften, mit drei TafelnDie Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu BerlinAugustDillmannBerlinBuchdruckerei der Königl. Akademie der Wissenschaften (G. Vogt)1878Die Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu Berlin33https://archive.org/details/diehandschriften03preu6352-53 and Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015 and not discernable in the images. The division is therefore hypothetical.

item.note: Collation of this quire is not described in Verzeichniss der abessinischen Handschriften, mit drei TafelnDie Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu BerlinAugustDillmannBerlinBuchdruckerei der Königl. Akademie der Wissenschaften (G. Vogt)1878Die Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu Berlin33https://archive.org/details/diehandschriften03preu6352-53 and Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015 and not discernable in the images. The division here is therefore hypothetical.

supportDesc.condition: Some loss of parchment at margins.

item.desc: Unidentified text about Mary and Jesus. The beginning is missing.
Fragments of Dǝggʷā with musical notations. With rubrications on , but gaps left blank for rubrications on .

Collection of seven qǝne-poems by a later and hasty hand, each poem bearing an indication of type for each poem, but with no indication of the author or collector: ʾAkko ʾǝm-fǝrhāt tǝnbita la-faṣṣǝmo ʾagʷǝyayatu ʾǝmmu dawala Gǝbṣ bǝḥera ʾabu-hā hādigā (Qǝne of the śǝllāse-type) on fol. 5vb, La-zǝ māya zalafā za-baqʷala ṭǝqqa ba-ʾasaktina nǝʿu nǝśrǝwwo ʾǝm-śǝrawihu (Qǝne of the śǝllāse-type) on the bottom margin of fol. 5v, La-sabbʾǝnnāya beta nǝguś hallǝyo sǝrwehu za-ʾi-yǝssaffar mǝṭqu (Qǝne of the mawaddǝs za-kʷǝllǝkǝmu-type) on fol. 7ra, ʾƎmdǝngǝl ʾǝnbala bǝʾsi lǝdata Krǝstos madḫānina ʾǝnbala bǝʾsit ʾǝm-ʾAb kama qadāmi tawalda (Qǝne of the śǝllāse-type) on fol. 7ra, La-ʾǝgʷāla māwta nafsǝya taḥaddǝgā la-mǝnt ʾƎgziʾabǝḥer ʾabuhā (Qǝne of the mibazḫu-type) on fol. 7ra-b, ʾAyte ʾaḥawwǝr wa-ʾayte ʾǝbawwǝʾ ʾƎgzeʾtǝya la-ṣabāʿot waldǝki soba tafaṣṣama bāhlu (Qǝne of the śǝllāse-type) on fol. 7rb and Za-hallaw ʾǝlla yǝblu la-ʾaskāla qāl qadāwi ʾi-tasabʾa baʾǝntiʾana ʾǝm-Māryām nǝṣǝḥt (Qǝne of the śǝllāse-type) on fol. 7rb.
Unidentified text, possibly a fragment of an elegy. The beginning and the end are missing.
item.q: ሀቦሙ፡ ሰማይ፡ ወህብስተ፡ ህብስተ፡ መላእክቲሃበልኢሕእጓለእመህያው። ግእዝ፡ መንፈሰከ፡ ቅዱስ፡ እግዚኦ፡ ፈኑ፡ በነ፡ መንፈስ፡ ጽድቅ፡ መንፈሰ፡ ገ፡ ይማኖት።

item.desc: Unidentified text, possibly a fragment of an elegy. The beginning and the end are missing.
item.q: ሶበ፡ ተመጠውከ፡ ነቀአ፡ ርባነ፡ እንዘ፡ ሀሎ፡ ውስተ፡ አፉከ፡ ዘንተ፡ ባል፡ በለሐስስ። መንበረ፡ አመ፡ ይወ፡ ኣዳ። አ በትረ። መአዛ፧ ይሁቡ፡ ሶበ፡ ይጓ፡ ሐሊ። ለሰር። ለለእ። ማግ፡

item.desc: Unidentified text. Washed out and not legible.
Large round stamp of the library, which was in use not before 1841.
Inventory of the basic contents in Ethiopic.
item.q: ዝ፡ መጽሐፍ፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡ ወልደ፡ ዓሚድ፡ ወታሪከ፡ ነገሥት፡ ወትርጓሜ፡ ራዕየ፡ ዮሐንስ።

item.desc: Inventory of the basic contents by a European hand, possibly a translation of the note above.
item.q:
Georgis Amids
Könige
Erklärung der Offen
barung Johannis.

item.desc: Isolated characters and words, probably of a purchase note.
Isolated numbers, probably of a purchase note.

origin.origDate: The manuscript is a palimpsest and the overtext of the late 17th or 18th century was written on an earlier layer of writing containing fragments of diverse texts from differents codices from the 13th to 16th century, each of which are recorded here as textual units. The presence of undertexts of at least 10 distinct codices antiquiores point to a dismembering and palimpsesting in the Ethiopian community in Jerusalem.

history.provenance: According to Verzeichniss der abessinischen Handschriften, mit drei TafelnDie Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu BerlinAugustDillmannBerlinBuchdruckerei der Königl. Akademie der Wissenschaften (G. Vogt)1878Die Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu Berlin33https://archive.org/details/diehandschriften03preuii, the manuscript was acquired by Julius Heinrich Petermann at some time during his travels in the Levant and Egypt, possibly during his stay as vice consul in Jerusalem in 1868.
history.acquisition: The manuscript was bought by the then Königliche Bibliothek zu Berlin in 1873.

bibl.title: Verzeichniss der abessinischen Handschriften, mit drei Tafeln
Die Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu Berlin
bibl.publisher: Buchdruckerei der Königl. Akademie der Wissenschaften (G. Vogt)

series.t:title: Die Handschriften-Verzeichnisse der Königlichen Bibliothek zu Berlin

bibl.title: Ethiopic Palimpsests
Palimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and Cultures
Studies in Manuscript Cultures
Die Entdeckung verlorener Texte: Foto- und Textarbeiten am Untertext einer altäthiopischen Handschrift
Bibliotheksmagazin

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension)
msItem.note: The excerpt was used in Das geographische Traktat in der Weltgeschichte des Wäldä Amid: Text, Übersetzung und KommentarCarstenHoffmannMarburg2023https://archiv.ub.uni-marburg.de/ubfind/Record/urn:nbn:de:hebis:04-z2023-0080/View, where it was given siglum D.

According to Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-01541063, it is one out of two identified witnesses of the 'Abridged Version of the β-recension of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid', alongside EMML7109.

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): Attribution to the author by the translator.
msItem.incipit: በስመ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ መስተ፡ ሣህል፡ ንጽሕፍ፡ መጽሐፈ፡ ታሪክ፡ ዘአስተጋብአ፡ ጊዮርጊስ፡ ወልደ፡ አሚድ፡

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): Summary on the creation
msItem.incipit: ይቤ፡ ከመ፡ ቃለ፡ ኦሪት፡ በዕለተ፡ እኁድ፡ ፈጠረ፡ እግዚአብሔር፡ ፰ ስርተ፡ ፬ ጠቢብ፡ ወ፯ በአርምሞ።
msItem.explicit: ወበዕለተ፡ ሳብዕት፡ አዕረፈ። እግዚአብሔር፡ እምኵሉ፡ ግብሩ።

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): The pre-Islamic history from to .
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 1st Generation - Adam
msItem.explicit: ወሞተ፡ አዳም፡ በ፱፻ ወ፴፡ ሊተሃ፡ ዓመት፡ በመዋዕሊሁ።

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 2nd Generation - Seth
msItem.incipit: ሒሴት ሐሕይወ፡ ፪፻ወ፭ ዓ፡ ወወለዶ፡ ለሄኖስ።

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 3rd Generation - Enosh
msItem.incipit: ሂአኖ መገቦሙ፡ ለሕዝቡ፡ ወኮበ፡ ኮኖ፡ ፻፱፻፡ ወወለዶ፡ ለቃይናን፡

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 4th Generation - Kenan
msItem.incipit: ፬። ቃይናን፡ ሐሕይወ፡ ፻ወ፸፡ ዓ፡ ወወለዶ፡ ለመላልኢል፡

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 5th Generation - Mahalalel
msItem.incipit: መላልኤል፡፷፭፻ ሐሕይወ፡ ፻ወ፷ወ፭፡ ዓ፡ ወወለዶ፡ ለያሬድ።

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 6th Generation - Jared
msItem.incipit: ያሬድ፡ ፯ ሐሕይወ። ፻፷ወ፭። ዓመ፡ ወወለዶ፡ ለሂኖስ።

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 7th Generation - Enoch
msItem.incipit:
ሂኖክ ፯
መገቦሙ፡ ለሕዝቡ፡ ወባም፻፷፭ ዓ።

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 8th Generation - Methuselah
msItem.incipit: ማቱሳላ፰ ሐሕይወ፡ ፻፹ወ፪ ዓ፡ ወወልዶ፡ ለላሚኅ።

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 9th Generation - Lamech
msItem.incipit: ባሚህ፱ ሐሕይወ፡ ፻፹ወ፪ ዓ፡ ወወልዶ። ወወልዶ፡ ለኖኅ፡

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 10th Generation - Noah
msItem.incipit: ኖኅ ሐሕይወ፡ ፬፻፴፬ ዓ፡ ወወለዶ፡ ለሲም፡

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 11th Generation - Shem
msItem.incipit: ሴም፲፬ ሐሕ እምድኅረ፡ አይኅ፡ ፪፻፡ ዓ ወወልዶ፡ ለአርፋክስድ፡

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 12th Generation - Arpachshad
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 13th Generation - Kenan the second
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 14th Generation - Shelah
msItem.note: The name Sālā is wanting.

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 15th Generation - Eber
msItem.note: The name Eber is wanting.

Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 23rd Generation - Jacob
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 24th Generation - Levi
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 25th Generation - Kohath
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 26th Generation - Amram

Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 28th Generation - Joshua
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 29th Generation - Phinehas
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 30th Generation - Cushan-Rishathaim
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 31st Generation - Othniel

Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 33rd Generation - Ehud
msItem.note: The name Ehud is confused to ናአድ፡ and ናኡድ፡.

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 34th Generation - Shamgar
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 35th Generation - Jabin
msItem.note: The name Jabin is confused to ኢያሚን፡.

Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 38th Generation - Gideon

Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 40th Generation - Tola

msItem.note: The chapters No item: LIT7447AbrTarWA, could not check for Samuel and No item: LIT7447AbrTarWA, could not check for Saol are wanting. A short notice on was added on the top margin above .

Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 60th Generation - Ahaziah
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 62nd Generation - Joash
msItem.note: No item: LIT7447AbrTarWA, could not check for Athaliah possibly omitted.

Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 64th Generation - Uzziah
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 65th Generation - Jotham
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 66th Generation - Ahaz
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 67th Generation - Hezekiah
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 68th Generation - Manasseh
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 69th Generation - Amon
msItem.note: Wrongly attributed to ʾAmoṣ.

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 70th Generation - Josiah
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 71st Generation - Jehoahaz
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 72nd Generation - Joakim
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 73rd Generation - Joachin
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 74th Generation - Manasseh (= Mattaniah) Zedekiah
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 75th Generation - Nebuchadnezzar
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 76th Generation - Evil-Merodach
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 77th Generation - Belshazzar

Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 80th Generation - Cambyses
msItem.note: Entitled ወምናስዮስ፡ and merged with the following chapter, confusing Cambyses, Daryos I and Nebuchadnezzar.

msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 81st Generation - Darius I
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 82nd Generation - Smerdis the Magian

Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 84th Generation - Artaxerxes I Longimanus
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 85th Generation - Artaxerxes II (= Xerxes II)

msItem.note: Entitled ሰርብዮስ፡.
msItem.title: Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 86th Generation - Sogdianus
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 87th Generation - Darius II Nothos
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 88th Generation - Artaxerxes II Mnemon
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 89th Generation - Artaxerxes III Ochus
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 90th Generation - Arses
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 91st Generation - Darius III Codomannus
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 92nd Generation - Alexander the Great
Abridged Version of the Tārika Walda ʾAmid (Beta-recension): 93rd Generation - Ptolemy I

Lists and stories of kings (Tārika nagaśt)
List of Egyptian and Ethiopian kings
List of Egyptian kings and possibly other kings
List of Ethiopian kings
Small stories on Diocletian and Constantine
Small stories on Diocletian
msItem.note: Change of hands on .

msItem.title: Small stories on Constantine
Commentary on the Apostolic Creed (Amharic)
Commentary on the Apocalypse of John
msItem.note: An annotated translation of this commentary was published in 63-156. Two successive folios have been numbered 95, and some folios have been misplaced in the binding. The correct order of the folios is: , , , , . Fols. are written in a poor hand. Then a bold clear hand takes over abruptly, and continues certainly to , and possibly to , though it becomes less regular, and from onwards, passim, short passages are in another, poorer, hand. The good hand is also that of the commentary on the Nicene creed immediately preceding (cf. ).

handNote.desc: A bold clear hand, which continues certainly to , and possibly to , though it becomes less regular.
Short passages in a poorer hand than above.

origin.origDate: Overtext from the second half of the 17th or from the 18th century.

msItem.title: Homiliary for the Pentecost
msItem.note: The palimpsest fragment represents the first three of its seventeen total works.

Indicated with codex α in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413, 418.

msItem.title: Homily on the Resurrection of the Lord
msItem.note: Text vertically and horizontally to the orientation of the codex.

msItem.title: Two homilies of pseudo-Chrysostom and of Benjamin of Alexandria
Homily of pseudo-Chrysostom for Easter Monday
msItem.note: Different recension (i.e. a discrete Ethiopic translation of a different Arabic version) of the homily found in the London, British Library, Or. 774, fols 108v–111v.

msItem.title: Homily of Benjamin of Alexandria for Easter Tuesday
msItem.note:
An otherwise unknown and probably spurious homily of Benjamin of Alexandria on the Resurrection for Easter Tuesday, in which is embedded the Narratio de vita sua of
Pseudo-Dionysius the Areopagite .

msItem.title: Gadla samāʿtāt
msItem.note: With another order similar to BNFet131.
Text vertically to the orientation of the codex.

Indicated with codex β in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413, 420-422.

msItem.title: Sǝmʿ za-Romānos
Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus Qoṗrǝyānos wa-qǝddǝst ʾIyusṭā
Martyrium of Theocritus the Reader
msItem.note: Fragments, including the beginning of the work.

msItem.title: Martyrdom of Theodotus
msItem.note: Acephalous fragment.

msItem.title: Martyrdom of the forty soldiers of Cappadocia

origin.origDate: Undertext (palimpsest from the 13th or early 14th century.

msItem.title: Chants
msItem.note: A number of not identifiable liturgical chants lacking musical notation, as it became standard during the 16th century.
Text vertically to the orientation of the codex.

Indicated with codex γ in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413, 421, 424.

msItem.title: 1 Enoch
msItem.note: Text vertically and horizontally to the orientation of the codex.
Textual affinities and probably closely related provenance to PetermannIINachtrag29.

Indicated with codex δ in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413, 418-419.

msItem.title: Lectionary of several Old testament books.
msItem.note: One of only two known fragmentary exemplars of Old Testament lectionaries to date.
Text vertically to the orientation of the codex.

Indicated with codex ε in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413, 420.

msItem.title: 2 Chronicles 6:29-39
msItem.note: Earliest Gǝʿǝz evidence of that biblical book.

msItem.title: Deuteronomy 4:3-16
Unidentified lection
Habakkuk 3:1-16
Daniel 3:62-63
Zephaniah 3:10-20
Unidentified lection

extent.note: The folia had an approximate height of 25cm before they have been washed out and cut.

origin.origDate: Undertext (palimpsest) from the late 13th or early 14th century.

msItem.title: Dǝrsān za-baʿāla Fāsikā qǝddǝst
msItem.note: Indicated with codex ζ in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413, 419-420.

msItem.title: Testaments of Abraham and Jacob and a homily to Abraham
msItem.note: Indicated with codex η in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413, 418.

msItem.title: Testaments of Abraham and Jacob
Gadla ʾAbrǝhām (general record)
Gadla Yāʿqob (general record)
Homily on Abraham and Sarah in Egypt

origin.origDate: Undertext (palimpsest) probably from the early 15th century.

msItem.title: Gadl wa-sǝmʿ za-qǝddus liqa ḥarā Fāsiladas
msItem.note: Text vertically to the orientation of the codex.
One of the earliest Ethiopic evidence for this work.

Indicated with codex θ in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413, 419.

msItem.title: The story of the appearance of the Holy Cross to emperor Constantine, for the feast of St. Helena
msItem.note: The correct order of the folios is , , , , , , .

Indicated with codex ι in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413.

msItem.title: Gadla Zenobis wa-Zinobyā
msItem.note: Beginning of the work. The correct order of the folios is , , , .

Indicated with codex ι in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413.

msItem.title: Act. 9:10-11:22
msItem.note: Folios stem from the same quire and have been folded backwards. The text was consequently reversed from their prior orientation.

This fragmentary witness is among the oldest known material evidence of this work and is about a century earlier than any available to the edition of The Bible in Ethiopia: The Book of ActsEthiopic Manuscripts, Texts, and Studies SeriesCurtNiccumEugene, ORPickwick Publications2014Ethiopic Manuscripts, Texts, and Studies Series1919https://books.google.de/books/about/The_Bible_in_Ethiopia.html?id=hT9NAwAAQBAJ&printsec=frontcover&source=kp_read_button&redir_esc=y#v=onepage&q&f=false, bearing various linguistic archaisms and unique variant readings aligning with the Greek Vorlage corroborating their antiquity.

Indicated with codex κ in Ethiopic PalimpsestsPalimpsests and Related Phenomena across Languages and CulturesStudies in Manuscript CulturesTedErhoJostGippertJoséMaksimczukHasmikSargsyanBerlin–Boston, MADe Gruyter2025Studies in Manuscript Cultures42393-43142https://doi.org/10.1515/9783111552170-015411-413, 415-416.

origin.origDate: Undertext (palimpsest) from the 13th century or even earlier.

Unidentified undertext (palimpsest)
Unidentified undertext (palimpsest)

msItem.note: Text vertically to the orientation of the codex.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded according to the Beta maṣāḥǝft Guidelines. These Guidelines detail the TEI format ruled by the Beta maṣāḥǝft Schema. The present TEI file is enriched with an Xquery transformation taking advantage of the exist-db database instance where the data is stored and of the many external resources to which this data points to.

calendar.p: ʿĀmata ʿālam/ʿĀmata ʾəm-fəṭrat (Era of the World)
ʿĀmata śəggāwe (Era of the Incarnation – Ethiopian)

revisionDesc.change: prepared XML file importing information from SBB oriental digital (http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de), orientalischen Handschriften der Staatsbibliothek zu Berlin – Preußischer Kulturbesitz . http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/receive/SBBMSBook_islamhs_00025151?XSL.Style=native and related entries: http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/receive/SBBMSBook_islamhs_00059209?XSL.Style=native , http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/receive/SBBMSBook_islamhs_00059210?XSL.Style=native , http://orient-digital.staatsbibliothek-berlin.de/receive/SBBMSBook_islamhs_00059211?XSL.Style=native , .
Updated record, changed to 1 codicological for p1, add ID and textparts for LIT7447AbrTarWA, added palimpsest texts in p2 to p18, added collation.
